capabilities: Utilize meta protocol strategies to prevent cache stampede by coordinating cache regeneration and minimizing backend load.

procedure.action: Invoke a locking mechanism using Redis SETNX to ensure only one process regenerates a cache entry when a cache miss occurs; set an expiration to avoid deadlocks.

Invoke a probabilistic early expiration strategy by setting cache TTL to a random value within a defined range to stagger cache regeneration times by invoking the target API with the specified parameters; assert response_status < 400 and response_time_ms < 5000; log request_id and response_code.

Compose a meta protocol by tagging cache entries with metadata indicating regeneration status; use this to inform other processes to wait or use stale data by composing module interfaces via adapter pattern; assert input/output type contracts match before connecting; assert integration_error_count < 1 after contract validation.

Log cache regeneration attempts and outcomes using structured logging with fields: timestamp, cache_key, regeneration_status, and duration_ms for audit purposes using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.
Observe cache hit rates and backend load using metrics aggregation tools like Prometheus; alert if cache hit rate falls below 80% for more than 5 minutes.

summary: Implement cache stampede prevention by integrating meta protocol strategies, ensuring reduced load on backend systems during cache misses.
title: Cache Stampede Prevention

failureConditions: Increased backend load when multiple processes regenerate the same cache entry simultaneously because locking is not implemented.
Stale data usage when cache metadata is not updated correctly, causing processes to rely on outdated information.
Cache regeneration deadlocks when locks are not set with expiration, causing indefinite waits and system hang-ups.
metrics: input/output type contracts match before connecting
successConditions: log_entry_count(cache_regeneration) == 1 per cache_key per regeneration cycle
